levels.title: Using Functions
levels.description: Learn how to use functions with and without inputs.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've completed your first level and learned how to use functions with and without inputs.
levels.milestone_content:
# You Did It! 🎉

You've completed your first level and taken your first steps into the world of programming!

## What you've learned:
- How to use functions to make things happen
- How to use functions without inputs
- How to use functions with inputs to control their behavior
- How to solve problems by breaking them down into steps

## Next steps:
- Continue to the next level to build on these skills
- Practice what you've learned
- Keep experimenting and having fun!

You're on your way to becoming a great programmer. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_subject: Congratulations on hitting the first Milestone
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work on completing the first level of Jiki's Coding Fundamentals course.

It might not feel like you're doing much "real" coding yet, but you've just picked up one of the most important skills in programming. You'll be using functions hundreds of times a day from here on, so this really is the foundation for everything else.

Next we'll look at using colours to draw, and then we'll start mixing some logic into the process. Have fun!

lessons.title: Welcome to Coding Fundamentals
lessons.description: Meet Jiki and learn the basics of how coding works.

lessons.title: Solve the Maze
lessons.description: Guide Jiki through a maze using simple instructions.

lessons.title: Using Functions
lessons.description: Learn about functions, the little machines that make things happen.

lessons.title: Space Invaders
lessons.description: Shoot down some aliens in your first taste of Space Invaders.

lessons.title: Functions Taking Inputs (Numbers)
lessons.description: Learn how to give extra information to functions.

lessons.title: Take a Walk
lessons.description: Navigate the maze using a function that walks multiple steps.

lessons.title: Fix the Wall
lessons.description: Patch up the gaps in a broken wall with rectangles.

lessons.title: Snowman Basic
lessons.description: Build a snowman from three circles.

levels.title: Strings and Colors
levels.description: Learn how to use strings and colors to make your programs more colorful and expressive.
levels.milestone_summary: Excellent work! You've learned how to use strings and colors to create colorful drawings.
levels.milestone_content:
# Great Progress! 🎨

You've mastered using strings and colors in your programs!

## What you've learned:
- How to use strings to work with text and names
- How to use colors to make your drawings more vibrant
- How to combine shapes and colors to create complex images

## Next steps:
- Continue building on these skills
- Create even more complex and colorful programs
- Keep experimenting with different combinations!

You're becoming a skilled programmer. Keep it up!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work on this level, and on your first proper bit of drawing.

You've now added strings and colours to your toolkit, which means your programs can start handling text and producing things that actually look good on screen.

Over the next few lessons we'll start bringing in some logic, so you can move beyond linear instructions and add some variety and intelligence into your programs. Have fun!

lessons.title: Strings
lessons.description: Learn how to use text in your programs.

lessons.title: Foxy Face
lessons.description: Build a geometric fox face from colourful triangles.

lessons.title: Penguin
lessons.description: Complete a symmetrical penguin drawing.

lessons.title: Cloud, Rain and Sun
lessons.description: Create a full weather scene with clouds, rain and sunshine.

lessons.title: Jumbled House
lessons.description: Rearrange the pieces of a jumbled-up house.

levels.title: Repeat Loop
levels.description: Learn how to repeat actions using loops to make your code more efficient.
levels.milestone_summary: Great job! You've learned how to use repeat loops to make your programs more powerful and efficient.
levels.milestone_content:
# Fantastic Work! 🔁

You've mastered the repeat loop - one of the most powerful tools in programming!

## What you've learned:
- How to use loops to repeat actions
- How to write more efficient code with less repetition
- How to solve problems that require doing the same thing multiple times

## Next steps:
- Apply loops to more complex challenges
- Combine loops with everything else you've learned
- Keep building your programming skills!

You're making excellent progress. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_subject: Feeling Loopy after all that Looping?
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work on this level. Loops are a fundamental building block of programming, and hopefully they're starting to feel pretty comfortable.

There are other types of loops we'll look at later in the course, but the repeat loop will be your companion for the next few lessons.

Next up are variables, which is the idea of storing and retrieving values as your program runs. Have fun!

lessons.title: Repeat Loop
lessons.description: Learn how to tell Jiki to do things multiple times.

lessons.title: Golf Rolling Ball Loop
lessons.description: Roll a golf ball into the hole using a loop.

lessons.title: Maze Solve Repeat
lessons.description: Refactor a maze solution to use loops instead of repeated code.

lessons.title: Space Invaders Repeat
lessons.description: Use loops to efficiently destroy a wave of aliens.

levels.title: Variables
levels.description: Learn how to store and reuse values using variables.
levels.milestone_summary: Excellent! You've learned how to use variables to store and reuse values in your programs.
levels.milestone_content:
# Outstanding Work! 📦

You've mastered variables - a fundamental building block of programming!

## What you've learned:
- How to create and use variables to store values
- How to make your code more flexible and reusable
- How to change values in one place and see results everywhere
- How variables make complex programs easier to manage

## Next steps:
- Use variables in more complex programs
- Combine variables with loops and functions
- Keep building your programming toolkit!

You're becoming a real programmer now. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work on getting variables under your belt.

Variables are one of those ideas that's pretty simple on the surface, but they're going to underpin almost everything you write from now on. The ability to store a value, give it a name, and use it later is what turns code from a fixed list of instructions into something genuinely flexible.

Next we'll look at how variables change over time, which is what programmers call "state". Have fun!

lessons.title: Creating/Using Variables
lessons.description: Learn how to save and retrieve information in your programs.

lessons.title: Snowman
lessons.description: Set the right variable values to build a snowman.

lessons.title: Traffic Light
lessons.description: Draw a traffic light using variables for position and size.

lessons.title: Arithmetic + Variables
lessons.description: Learn how to do maths with variables.

lessons.title: Relational Sun
lessons.description: Position a sun where everything's calculated from variables.

lessons.title: Relational Snowman
lessons.description: Rebuild your snowman so all sizes scale from one variable.

lessons.title: Relational Traffic Lights
lessons.description: Rebuild your traffic light so everything scales together.

levels.title: Basic State
levels.description: Learn how to update variables and track changing values over time.
levels.milestone_summary: Amazing! You've learned how to use variables to track state and create dynamic programs.
levels.milestone_content:
# Incredible Progress! 📈

You've mastered using variables to track state - a crucial programming skill!

## What you've learned:
- How to update variables to track changing values
- How to use state to make your programs dynamic
- How to combine all your skills to create complex programs

## Next steps:
- Apply state management to more complex challenges
- Keep experimenting and having fun!

You're well on your way to mastering programming. Keep it up!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work on this one. You can now track values that change as your program runs, which is a real step up from where you were a couple of levels ago.

This idea of state is going to keep coming back throughout the course, and it gets more interesting every time we combine it with something new.

Next is a meaty level. We'll look at functions that return values, and then explore colours, randomness, animation, scope, and nested loops along the way. Have fun!

lessons.title: Updating Variables
lessons.description: Learn how to change variable values as your code runs.

lessons.title: Plant the Flowers
lessons.description: Plant a row of flowers across the garden, one by one.

lessons.title: Rolling Ball
lessons.description: Roll a golf ball into the hole by tracking its position.

lessons.title: Finish the Wall
lessons.description: Lay a row of bricks using a loop and a position variable.

levels.title: Functions That Return Things
levels.description: Learn how functions can return values, and explore colors, animation, randomness, scope, scenarios, and nested loops.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've mastered functions that return things and explored many powerful programming concepts.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Functions That Return Things level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Brilliant work on a packed level. Functions that return values, HSL and RGB colours, animation, random numbers, scope, scenarios, and loops within loops. That's a lot of new ideas to add to your toolkit in one go.

Don't worry if some of them still feel a bit hazy. They'll all come back in different contexts later in the course, and each time they'll get more familiar.

Next we'll start making decisions in code using if statements. This is where your programs really start to feel intelligent. Have fun!

lessons.title: Functions That Return Things
lessons.description: Learn how functions can give information back to you.

lessons.title: DND Roll
lessons.description: Roll some dice and strike a goblin in a D&D adventure.

lessons.title: Gold Panning
lessons.description: Pan for gold nuggets in a river and sell what you find.

lessons.title: Colors (HSL and RGB)
lessons.description: Learn different ways to specify exact colours.

lessons.title: Rainbow
lessons.description: Paint a rainbow from 100 colourful vertical bars.

lessons.title: Animation
lessons.description: Learn how to make things move on screen.

lessons.title: Sunset
lessons.description: Animate a beautiful sunset scene.

lessons.title: Random Numbers
lessons.description: Learn how to add randomness to your programs.

lessons.title: Random Salad
lessons.description: Toss together a random salad from different ingredients.

lessons.title: Scope
lessons.description: Learn what happens to variables inside and outside of code blocks.

lessons.title: Rainbow Splodges
lessons.description: Splatter hundreds of colourful splodges across the canvas.

lessons.title: Stock Market
lessons.description: Track your investment as the stock market randomly rises and falls.

lessons.title: Scenarios
lessons.description: Learn how to run your code with different starting conditions.

lessons.title: Golf Shot Scenarios
lessons.description: Roll a ball to the correct spot.

lessons.title: Owner's Bouquets
lessons.description: Plant flowers in different garden layouts.

lessons.title: Skyscraper
lessons.description: Build a skyscraper on the city grid.

lessons.title: Loops in Loops
lessons.description: Learn how to put loops inside other loops.

lessons.title: Space Invaders Nested Repeat
lessons.description: Fill the screen with rows and columns of aliens, then destroy them all.

levels.title: Conditionals
levels.description: Learn how to make decisions in your code using if statements and else clauses.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned how to use conditionals to make decisions in your programs.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Conditionals level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Nice work on the conditionals level. Hopefully if and else are starting to feel pretty natural.

Conditionals are one of the most important ideas in programming. Almost every interesting program needs to make decisions based on what's true at the time, and you've now got the basic tools to do that.

Next we'll look at more powerful ways of writing conditions, combining them together with and/or, plus a few related ideas like modulo. Have fun!

lessons.title: If Statements
lessons.description: Learn how to make your code do different things in different situations.

lessons.title: Bouncer
lessons.description: Decide who's old enough to get into the club.

lessons.title: Space Invaders Conditionals
lessons.description: Only shoot when there's an alien in your sights.

lessons.title: Else and Else If
lessons.description: Learn how to handle multiple different conditions.

lessons.title: Bouncer Wristbands
lessons.description: Give clubbers the right coloured wristband based on their age.

lessons.title: Digital Clock
lessons.description: Display the correct time on a 12-hour digital clock.

levels.title: Complex Conditionals
levels.description: Learn about logical operators, modulo, and more advanced conditional patterns.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've mastered complex conditionals and logical operators.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Complex Conditionals level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work on a tricky level. You've now got and/or, modulo, and looping without a count under your belt.

These are the kinds of tools that let you write conditions that actually match real-world logic, rather than having to break everything down into tiny separate checks.

Next we'll combine conditionals with state, which is where your programs start being able to respond to what's happening as they run. Have fun!

lessons.title: Logic Gates
lessons.description: Learn how to combine conditions together.

lessons.title: Rock, Paper, Scissors
lessons.description: Determine the winner of a Rock Paper Scissors game.

lessons.title: Bouncer Dress Code
lessons.description: Check both age and outfit before letting people in.

lessons.title: Shot Checker
lessons.description: Work out whether a golf shot landed close enough to sink.

lessons.title: Modulo and Not
lessons.description: Learn about remainders and how to flip conditions.

lessons.title: Stripey Fabric
lessons.description: Weave a striped pattern by choosing each stripe's colour with modulo and logical-or.

lessons.title: Annalyn's Infiltration
lessons.description: Combine and, or, and not to decide which quest actions Annalyn can take.

lessons.title: Repeat Without an Input
lessons.description: Learn how to loop without knowing how many times upfront.

lessons.title: Solve the Maze Programatically
lessons.description: Write code that navigates any maze by itself.

levels.title: Conditionals and State
levels.description: Learn how to combine conditionals with state to create dynamic, responsive programs.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned to combine conditionals and state together.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Conditionals and State level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work. Combining conditionals with state is where your programs really start to feel alive, responding to what's happening rather than just following a fixed script.

This is roughly the level of complexity you'll be writing at for the rest of the course, so everything you've practised here will keep coming back.

Next up is a big one: writing your own functions. Up until now you've been using functions someone else wrote. From here on, you'll be writing your own. Have fun!

lessons.title: State
lessons.description: Learn how to combine conditions with changing data.

lessons.title: Build the Wall
lessons.description: Build a wall from scratch, brick by brick, row by row.

lessons.title: Scroll and Shoot
lessons.description: Scroll through Space Invaders and take out the aliens.

levels.title: Make Your Own Functions
levels.description: Learn how to write your own functions with inputs and return values.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned to write your own functions.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Make Your Own Functions level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Brilliant work. Writing your own functions is a real shift. You're no longer just using the tools on the shelf, you're building new ones.

This is one of the most important skills in all of programming. Good code is mostly about breaking problems down into small, well-named functions, and you've now got everything you need to start doing that.

Next we'll get back to strings and look at how to stick them together and slot values into them. Have fun!

lessons.title: Writing Your Own Functions
lessons.description: Learn how to create your own reusable machines.

lessons.title: Turn Around
lessons.description: Create a turn-around function for the maze.

lessons.title: Battle Procedures
lessons.description: Extract your shooting logic into a reusable function.

lessons.title: Adding Inputs to Your Functions
lessons.description: Learn how to make your functions accept inputs.

lessons.title: Learn to Walk
lessons.description: Create a walk function that moves multiple steps at once.

lessons.title: Adding Returns to Your Functions
lessons.description: Learn how to make your functions give back results.

lessons.title: Even or Odd
lessons.description: Write a function that works out whether a number is even or odd.

lessons.title: Triangle
lessons.description: Classify a triangle as equilateral, isosceles or scalene.

lessons.title: Leap Year
lessons.description: Work out whether a year is a leap year.

lessons.title: Collatz Conjecture
lessons.description: Explore a famous maths puzzle with your own function.

lessons.title: Look Around
lessons.description: Build the functions that check what's around you in the maze.

levels.title: String Manipulation
levels.description: Learn how to concatenate strings and use template literals.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned to manipulate strings.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the String Manipulation level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Nice work on this one. Concatenation and templates are pretty simple ideas, but they come up constantly, especially anywhere you're producing output for a person to read.

You'll be using these techniques in pretty much every program you write from here on.

Next we'll look at how to dig into strings character by character, and how to loop through their contents. Have fun!

lessons.title: String Concatenation and Templates
lessons.description: Learn how to join bits of text together.

lessons.title: Hello
lessons.description: Say hello to the world with your first string function.

lessons.title: Two-Fer
lessons.description: Share some cake: "One for you, one for me."

lessons.title: Raindrop Sounds
lessons.description: Turn numbers into raindrop sounds: Pling, Plang and Plong.

levels.title: String Iteration
levels.description: Learn how to index into and iterate through strings.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned to iterate through strings.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the String Iteration level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work. Hopefully indexing and looping through strings is starting to make sense.

Being able to look at individual characters and walk through text systematically opens up a huge range of problems you can now solve. A lot of real-world programming is about exactly this kind of work.

Next we'll look at how to combine multiple functions together to tackle more complex tasks. Have fun!

lessons.title: String Indexing
lessons.description: Learn how to access individual characters in text.

lessons.title: Three Letter Acronym
lessons.description: Build an acronym from the first letter of three words.

lessons.title: Iterating Through Strings
lessons.description: Learn how to go through text one character at a time.

lessons.title: Contains
lessons.description: Search through a rack of tiles to find a specific letter.

lessons.title: Index Of
lessons.description: Find exactly where a letter sits on the tile rack.

lessons.title: Sign Painter Price
lessons.description: Calculate the cost of painting a sign, letter by letter.

lessons.title: Reverse String
lessons.description: Flip a piece of text backwards.

lessons.title: Driving Test
lessons.description: Mark a driving theory test and decide if they passed.

lessons.title: Hamming
lessons.description: Count the differences between two DNA strands.

levels.title: Using Multiple Functions Together
levels.description: Learn how to combine multiple functions to solve complex problems.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned to use multiple functions together.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Using Multiple Functions Together level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work on this level. Combining functions to solve bigger problems is what professional programming actually looks like day to day. You break a problem into pieces, write a small function for each piece, and then put them together.

You're now doing this for real.

Next we'll look at methods and properties, which is a slightly different way of working with values. Have fun!

lessons.title: Using Multiple Functions Together
lessons.description: Learn how to combine functions to solve bigger problems.

lessons.title: Niche Named Party
lessons.description: Work out who's allowed into a very exclusive party.

lessons.title: Simple Pangram
lessons.description: Check if a sentence uses every letter of the alphabet.

levels.title: Methods and Properties
levels.description: Learn about methods and properties on objects.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned about methods and properties.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Methods and Properties level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Nice work. Methods and properties are a slightly different style to what you've seen so far. Instead of passing a value into a function, you call a function on the value itself. The idea takes a little getting used to, but it's used everywhere, so it'll start feeling natural pretty quickly.

Next we'll look at some more powerful kinds of loops: for loops, while loops, break, and continue. Have fun!

lessons.title: Methods and Properties
lessons.description: Learn how to use built-in tools that come attached to data.

lessons.title: Pangram
lessons.description: Check if a sentence contains every letter of the alphabet, regardless of case.

lessons.title: Nucleotide
lessons.description: Count how many times a specific nucleotide appears in a DNA strand.

levels.title: Advanced Loops
levels.description: Learn about for loops, continue, break, and other advanced loop concepts.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Advanced Loops level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Great work. You've now got for loops, while loops, break, and continue under your belt. These are the loops you'll reach for most often outside of this course, so they're well worth the time you've put in.

Each one has a slightly different job, and getting a feel for which to reach for will come with practice.

Next we'll look at lists, which is how programs store and work with collections of things. Have fun!

lessons.title: Advanced Loops
lessons.description: Learn proper JavaScript loops with more control and flexibility.

lessons.title: ISBN Verifier
lessons.description: Check whether a book's ISBN number is valid.

lessons.title: Alphanumeric
lessons.description: Build functions to classify text as letters, numbers or both.

levels.title: Lists
levels.description: Learn how to work with arrays to store and manipulate collections of data.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned to work with lists and arrays.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Lists level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
Brilliant work. Lists (or arrays, as a lot of programmers call them) are one of the most useful tools in any language. Pretty much any time you're dealing with more than one of something, you'll reach for a list.

You've now got a good grasp of how to read from them, build them up, and work through their contents.

Next, and finally, we'll look at dictionaries. These let you store values against named keys rather than just positions. Have fun!

lessons.title: Arrays
lessons.description: Learn how to store collections of things in lists.

lessons.title: Symbols Part 1
lessons.description: Map weather descriptions to the right combination of symbols.

lessons.title: Symbols Part 2
lessons.description: Draw weather scenes from lists of elements.

lessons.title: Guest List
lessons.description: Check if someone's name is on the guest list.

lessons.title: After Party
lessons.description: Check the VIP list when celebrities only give their first name.

lessons.title: Formal Dinner
lessons.description: Match guests to the dinner list using their honorific and surname.

lessons.title: Building Arrays
lessons.description: Learn how to build up lists piece by piece.

lessons.title: Pack a Lunch
lessons.description: Fill a lunchbox with the right combination of items.

lessons.title: Stars
lessons.description: Build a growing pattern of stars.

lessons.title: Meal Prep
lessons.description: Work out which ingredients you need to buy for your recipe.

lessons.title: Wordle: Process Guess
lessons.description: Check a Wordle guess and work out which letters are correct.

lessons.title: Extract Words
lessons.description: Pull individual words out of a sentence.

lessons.title: The Chop Shop
lessons.description: Work out if the barber has time for one more customer.

lessons.title: Wordle: Play a Game
lessons.description: Process a whole Wordle game, colouring each guess row by row.

levels.title: Dictionaries
levels.description: Learn how to use dictionaries to store key-value pairs.
levels.milestone_summary: Congratulations! You've learned to work with dictionaries.
levels.milestone_content:
# Level Complete!

You've completed the Dictionaries level. Keep going!
levels.milestone_email_content_markdown:
And that's the last level of Coding Fundamentals. Hopefully dictionaries are feeling like a useful tool to add to the mix alongside lists.

This is a real achievement. You've now covered every core building block of programming: functions, variables, state, conditionals, loops, strings, lists, and dictionaries. Everything else from here is combinations and refinements of what you already know.

We're really proud of you for getting this far. Have fun!

lessons.title: Dictionaries
lessons.description: Learn how to store data with named labels.

lessons.title: Lookup Time
lessons.description: Look up the current time for a city.

lessons.title: Scrabble Score
lessons.description: Calculate the score for a Scrabble word.

lessons.title: Changing Dictionaries
lessons.description: Learn how to update and modify dictionaries.

lessons.title: RNA Transcription
lessons.description: Convert a DNA sequence into its RNA complement.

lessons.title: Protein Translation
lessons.description: Translate RNA sequences into proteins.

lessons.title: Spotify Data
lessons.description: Parse your listening data from Spotify.

lessons.title: Word Count
lessons.description: Count how many times each word appears in a sentence.

lessons.title: LLM Response
lessons.description: Parse and format the response from an AI chatbot.

lessons.title: Nucleotide Count
lessons.description: Count all the different nucleotides in a DNA strand.
